content: Very rude staff and had no curtesy to old age passengers. Return flight was cancelled with no explanation and prior notice. When arrived airport (DEL) they try to accommodate in different flights and change about 3 times. I had no time to wait and ask them to change me to United airlines to EWR which was flying at about same time but they refused and said united has no seats available and it’s too late to book as it was 10 pm and flight was at 11:55. I asked United counter that if they have any seats and if I can book a ticket now to board today’s flight. The attendant was so helpful and said yes. I did book with them and flew on time . I canceled my ticket with American Airlines and will try never to fly with them again. Agent at counter was getting rude saying if I don’t want to change to other airlines they suggested about 5 hours of waiting and one stop than I can wait till next day flight. No value of customers time and urgency. Only select American if you are retired and nothing to do at your destination.

I flew from LFT to CDG with a connection in Dallas. The first flight was delayed multiple times so I missed the connection and had to rebook. It happens. When I got to Paris on the rebooked flight, it was over an hour late. The problem is that I had a train reservation and had to change the reservation, which costs money. Then I had to book an entirely new train due to the delay. AA said they would reimburse up to 200 USD, which does not cover the cost of these changes, then they came back again and said they wouldn't. I'll be sure not to book with them again. Better to go with United or Delta.

Don't travel with American Airlines if: - You have somewhere to be at a specific time if It's an vacation / event / holiday / work trip. I departed Nov 3, 2025 on flight AA 2325 from CHS to DFW leaving around 4:00pm PST, about 40 minutes after the scheduled 3:21pm departure time. Not a big issue. My connecting flight from DFW to SFO was flight AA 1430 departing at 7pm. However, American Airlines app did ping me saying that I could adjust my flight and take a later flight for no charge due to the delay. Very nice of them, right? I decided to hold because after reaching cruising altitude, the pilot informed us that we'd land at 5:45pm - plenty of time to make my 7pm flight. So as we're approaching DFW, I get more notifications from the AA app that they're offering travel credits - up to $650 - to take a later flight because they overbooked the flight I was connecting to. Having work meetings early all day the next day, I really didn't want to take the next flight getting me in at midnight PST (3am EST - where I was traveling from).  we land at DFW at 5:45pm - as the pilot mentioned, and they say we "don't have a gate". We proceed to sit there, and sit there. At 6:15pm (5 minutes before my flight starts boarding) I flagged down a steward to ask if the pilot and crew have informed DFW that we're still waiting on a gate and trying to make our connecting flights. He just smiled at me and said they're doing the best they can. At 6:30PM we started deplaning. I'm only one terminal away - going from Gate A28 to Gate B9 (which is right off the escalator stairs) I sprint to the train, to the gate and arrive at 6:53pm. And of course, they had the boarding doors closed without an agent insight. So I had to go thru the trouble of rebooking the flight, contacting AA, and then get a flight 3 hours later because they 1) didn't want to give flight credits and 2) oversold the connecting flight. Here's the kicker, I had paid for a better seat for the flight they overbooked. Didn't apologize, didn't reach out post, and didn't even offer to refund the price of the seat. Thank God for Delta.

I’ve flown with this airline many times, and every trip starts the same way with hope. I book my ticket, plan my schedule, and trust that things will go smoothly. Then the email comes: Your flight has been canceled. It has become a pattern. Every time I book, the flight disappears from the schedule, and I’m left scrambling to rebook. The worst part is that the new flight always costs more. It feels like I’m being punished for their mistake. I spend hours on the phone waiting for someone to help. The agents are polite, but there is no real solution. They offer credits or apologies, but the price difference is on me. A refund does not help when I still need to travel and every replacement flight is more expensive. Each cancellation throws my plans into chaos. I have missed meetings, lost reservations, and added stress. Travel should not feel like this. Customers deserve honesty, fair prices, and reliable service. I am not asking for perfection, only consistency. If the airline cancels a flight, it should honor the original price when we rebook. That is fair. That is decent. Until that happens, I will think twice before booking again.

We flew with AA for the first time since the 90's LHR to CLT and upon arrival at CLT we went to get our cases. They came out and look as if someone had run them over. We approached the American Airlines staff who were seated by the luggage belt and they too were shocked and confirmed this was unacceptable. Their advice was to take photos as evidence along with a photo of the luggage belt as we could not raise our complaint claim until we reach our final destination (TYS) via flight AA3119 from (CLT to TYS). They were kind enough to aid us in taking the photo's to ensure they were sufficient for the claim. At McGhee Tyson Airport in Knoxville (TYS) initially the American Airlines baggage office was closed, however after waiting and asking several staff members at the airport someone arrived and took us through the process. We both filled out a PIR form (or complaint procedure via the QR code as provided by the American Airlines staff member) and thought that would be all that was required as again the staff member there told us that the cases look bad and were clearly miss treated so we left for the hotel in out hire car. Shockingly it seemed as if the claims had not even been looked at as the next morning we woke to two emails (received through the night) saying that the review of the files is complete and that they don’t cover, and I quote “minor scratches, stains or dents”. Obviously we very unhappy with the replies and got onto the American Airlines Chat service via their app where I was asked to submit my refusal of this decision via another form. After about 1.5hrs chatting we finally finished this and the new claims were raised on 09/09/2025. We submitted all the same evidence and the chat conscript as additional evidence as we were advised they would get in touch asap as they need to look into things better. To our surprise American Airlines never replied. We finished a 10 day holiday, flew home and still nothing. I emailed their complaints dept and cc'd their CEO office and was ignored again. They really are a disgusting company who take no accountability for their actions. We will never fly with this airline again, I would rather a 4hr drive than use them and a connecting flight.

We recently traveled on American Airlines with our two grandchildren, ages 1 and 3. It was a nightmare. We were diverted to another airport due to the pilot "timing out". We waited on the tarmac for 1.5 hrs. for a new one to show. By this time, our original destination had closed customs so they sent us into this airport for customs at 11:00 pm. The airport wasn't expecting this flight so they didn't know where our luggage was and didn't have our names in the system to reboard. Another two hours on the tarmac and we felt we were going to leave when they announced the flight attendents had now "timed out" and we would have to wait for a new crew. During our 6 hours on the tarmac, they could not provide water or snacks because the flight was not expected to be there and they couldn't get supplies "restocked"? All the babies on the flight were crying, one lady passed out and the passengers were crying. By the time we got to our actual destination airport (9 hours after taking off) the airport was a ghost town and we couldn't get our luggage including the babies car seats! Therefore we couldn't go anywhere to get a room to sleep - it was 2 am! Needless to say, I contacted the airline for a refund and went through many rejections to get to the top of the customer service department and all they would give me was 7,500 points/miles.

London to Austin with British Airways. American Airlines promised me a refund and then made it imposssible for customers to contact them. The only phone number AA will give you is to book a flight. All other contacts are thru forms online or email. If you comply, they just ignore you.. There is no way to contact them. They owe me money, but I don't know how to contact them. They do not allow you to call the right departments.

Flight AA3102 - Logan Boston to DFW - A321 - seats 34a & 34b. Second return flight with AA for connection to DFW - Syd. Newer and much improved A321 aircraft (from outbound on older A320). Fantastic service from gate attendant at Boston to accommodate having side by side seating from assigned separate seating - full aircraft. Ground services and boarding well organised and group boarding enforced. The A321 is a significant improvement on the A320, much newer and aesthetically more pleasing environment, - seating better & more comfortable, seat back better for devices, similar good leg spacing, vastly improved overhead storage with bigger bins so less hassle with carry on luggage. Flight crew again very good but no quite as personable as out bound funny ladies. Again AA provided light refreshments with good variety of beverages (including alcohol) Moose Beer etc and small selection of snacks. Ordered from the very limited additional cost menu and snack was nice. AA's on board free wifi entertainment suite was again pretty good and worked flawlessly. Slight flight delay out of Boston but to AA's credit arrived safely and on time at DFW. Again overall great service from AA and substantial improvement over first leg. Would be happy to use AA for similar shorter flights (especially if on A321).

Awful airline. Seats incredibly cramped, both for leg room and width. Staff rude and inattentive. Online check in didn’t work so unable to select seats. Preordered veggie meal not available. It was cheap though and on time.

Do not fly with American Airlines! I went to Jamaica from Sept 26 to Sept 30 for my boyfriend's birthday. They destroyed my luggage and broke two wheels and ripped my boyfriend's luggage, and not only are they refusing to pay for the damages, but they are making the process for receiving a travel credit or some type of reimbursement impossible. We've had our bags for over a decade, so there's no receipt or bank statement available. I am not giving up, but this was absolutely ridiculous. Save yourself the trouble and book a flight with another airline!

My family and I booked 3 round trip tickets from Ft. Lauderdale to Bangkok. The flight was scheduled to leave on Aug.18 at 5:20 pm. arriving in Bangkok on the morning of Aug. 20. The flight was delayed until 12:15 a.m Aug. 19. Between arriving early as demanded by American Airlines we spent 9 hours waiting while being told hourly that the flight was getting ready to leave. When the plane that was to carry us to our 1st stop arrived both the pilot and co-pilot walked off the job.They could've called the tower and told them hours ago. This caused us to miss our next 2 flights and had us arriving a day later. It was a horrible ordeal. We paid for a hotel in Korea out of pocket along with cab fare both ways and meals. Some vouchers were offered but they were either too late or not accepted. I was told that I should have gotten flight insurance or more expensive seats then maybe they would do something. They acted like we were bothering them to reschedule us. Why do I need to have insurance when it was a labor dispute with the airline staff? Can an airline book a ton of flights and then when the staff refuses to fly they still keep the money? Simply put, with all the waiting, scrambling around to reschedule flights, exhaustion and missing deadlines in Bangkok and then to be told here's 50 bucks and some points that add up to nothing, too bad ....I hate this company, don't use them, they are not customer service oriented, stay away.

This is our second overseas America flight for this trip. Both flights were delayed. I understand that can happen, what I don’t understand is why the airline is not transparent. In Dallas the countdown to boarding continued when there clearly was no airplane at the gate. We are currently in Dublin and the flight is supposed to leave in 15 minutes and a whole lot of people are standing around(no seating available at this gate) waiting. Why can’t there just be an update? Just say, “we’re delayed for… or we dont know how long, etc”. I don’t understand the lack of communication/transparency.

My wife and I flew first-class on American from Orlando to San Francisco via Dallas Fort-Worth. We pre-ordered our meals and thus did not bring any food with us. After an hour into the flight, we were very hungry. I asked the attendant when meals would be served. She informed me that someone forgot to load the food so there would be none. She then directed me to call "corporate" and complain to them. We did not see any flight attendants during the rest of the flight. They spent their time chatting with each other in the front. after I returned home, I contacted American Airlines customer service and requested a partial refund. I received an apology and offer of a minimal amount of airline miles instead. I had heard that American is known for sub-standard first-class service. I now know this to be true.

I flew American Airlines business class from Los Angeles to Sydney and the experience was ‘ho-hum’. The flight was delayed by about an hour, and the flight attendants were a motley crew, and like the aircraft we were on – they were old and tired. Any request was bothersome and rebuked. When I was handed the glass of champagne I requested after take-off, I made the mistake of asking, what is it? This response was savage – ‘Champagne! To which I replied – Yes, I know, but what champagne is it? The meal came with 2 salads (I can’t work that out). I had pre-ordered the main dish of beef. This came with a side of mac 7 cheese – so pedestrian. The wagyu beef sliders offered as an in-flight snack sounded great, but were served at room temperature and tasteless. When I asked for an additional pillow, I was told there was only one extra pillow on the aircraft and that it had already been given to another passenger. Breakfast was a lacklustre affair – given America’s obsession with coffee and proper coffee-making machines are readily available - why is this airline still serving that terrible brewed stuff that tastes like dishwater. Maybe a fresh intake of flight attendants will do the trick.

AA 1048 DFW to LGN in economy - AA were the second leg of our trip as code share with QF7 out of SYD. AA got the job done but with no fanfare or polish. The flight was slighty delayed out of DFW with some expected issue at Boston. The A321 was well used and definitely showing age and wear/tear. Gate service and boarding were pretty good and staff stayed on track with boarding groups. Full flight. Seating comfort was lacking but legroom reasonable in economy. No on seat entertainment but credit to AA with a decent selection using their free WiFi app ( worked well) Crew on board very experienced (ie older) were friendly and one lady was an absolute delight, very bright and cheery and great on the chat with all passengers. Good range of drinks and a few very light snacks served on the 4hr trip. Flight crew did a great job getting us to Boston safely. Overall decent experience. (Would rate a little higher if aircraft was a bit better).

First time flying American Airlines in years. I have 7 delays total and have reboard 2 different airline on my last flight. Very unprofessional and unethical. No information was given regarding why we were having issues. We have to switch to a 2nd airline only to experience. I was suppose to arrive in Knoxville at 11:15. Currently I am still on the aircraft in knoxville, it is now 12:45 and we stuck on the runway. The captain says due to other planes and Airlines moving we are grounded till further notice on the runway. They want us to ensure that fly with them again.

Reno-Dallas-Barcelona. Departing flight had a slight delay but nothing of concern. Arrived at DFW in plenty of time for connecting flight to Barcelona. Mostly on time flight. Boarding was OK. Flight was full. Service began once in the air. Food was OK and beverage service was fine. Entertainment system worked well with plenty of choices. Lights out during the night which allowed me about 3 hours of sleep which I never do, so that was a plus. Light snack in morning before arrival and again a good beverage service. Arrival in Barcelona went well. Pretty quick immigration. All baggage arrived in a reasonable amount of time. Quick line for a taxi and we were on our way. I tend to have pretty good luck with my long-haul flights on American so nothing negative to say.

The flight was fine; however, they offered an in-flight special of 70,000 bonus miles for signing up for their Advantage program Mastercard with Barclay Bank in April of this year. When I later looked into using these miles, I discovered I only had 1,000 miles in my account! I reached out to them on August 23, again on September 3rd, after they failed to "call me back after we researched the matter". In both instances, they stated I had not "met the criteria" for these miles. I was subsequently told I “Didn’t meet the requirement of paying the annual fee of $99". It turns out they billed me for the annual fee in May; I assumed it would be added to my statement, standard for credit cards. After sharing my story with several customer representatives, I was told bluntly by a supervisor on September 12th, that "it is up to the customer to meet the requirements for the points." Please be aware that seeking to trick people out of their miles may not be uncommon, and it could happen to you. Fortunately, I found my copy of the annual fee charged in May.  This is plain wrong; I only signed up for their credit card because of the special offer of these bonus points.

This whole trip was a nightmare thanks to American. This was my second cancelled flight. Flying out west, my first flight (JAX to DFW) was cancelled with no available flight until the next morning. I had to get a hotel (no voucher offered by American) , no food voucher, no water even. Finally the next morning, my flight to Miami went fine and I arrived in Vegas a day late. On the return trip, my flight from Vegas to DFW was delayed, delayed, delayed then delayed to the next morning. Again, another hotel room (no hotel, food vouchers or water offered by American), arriving home a day later and cost of boarding my dog another day. So I go to the airport the next morning and it starts again. My flight from Vegas to DFW delayed, delayed, delayed, Finally I get to Dallas and am awaiting my flight to JAX. Delayed, delayed, delayed...again. This is the worst part: finally the flight is scheduled to leave and I am at the gate. The incoming flight arrives and after the last passenger gets off the plane the flight is cancelled and there is no other flight until the next morning. Are you serious??? I am a healthcare provider and could not be delayed another day, so I (and many others on this flight) flew Delta (almost same route) with NO weather issues. So they blamed all these cancellations on weather but it's a funny thing to me that my luggage arrived that very same night in JAX from DFW. I complained to American and all they said was "we can't control the weather" and gave me 7500 miles which is near nothing as you can't even complete a flight on those miles. I have recommended American and complimented them over the years I have been flying but no more. They have really changed and have zero respect for their returning customers. They also have no respect for any passengers as they never once offered us a hotel or smacks or even water.

My wife and I brought carry on luggage so we would not have the hassle of checking our luggage. While boarding, the attendants said we had to check our bags due to the storage bins being full. Once we boarded the plane we realized that the flight attendants allowed more than half the travelers to put their personal items (book bags & purses) in the overhead compartments, taking up space that the other travelers who HAD to check their bags could have used. Now instead of getting off the plane and heading home, we have to go to baggage claim and wait for our luggage that we should have been able to store on the plane.

Booked a flight to Kumamoto Japan from Cedar Rapids, IA. Booked on the American Airlines website. Since I am elderly and tall, I need extra legroom. I am a retired teacher, travelling to see my Son, and can’t afford to pay for business class, but I always purchase premium seats for the long flights. I was not able to purchase these seats because American Airlines had assigned me to a Japan Airlines flight from Chicago to Tokyo. JAL told me that American Airlines would assign me such seats. Had to cancel the flights. In the past few days the prices have gone up by about double. American has given me 10,000 extra miles. I have three more flights already booked with American in the next five months. My time flying on this airline is over. I will pay for being tall. I will not be crammed in for 13 hours and deal with rude and greedy people.

American Airlines is pathetic. I knew when I went through Expedia I could not get Delta. Hence American. They sent from Traverse City Michigan to Charlotte. Then to Seattle. Check that flight pattern. Got to Charlotte and then the fun began delayed almost 4 hours and still not sure if it's not delayed longer.

I was delayed out of DFW to San Diego, over an hour. I spoke to gate representative and was told it was due to maintenance, no other flights available. I called AA and spoke to 3 representatives for over 90m, on two occasions, I asked to speak to supervisor and they hung up on me, one representative told me "it's only an hour delay". I'm missing a connecting flight out of San Diego and they don't care one bit, got a generic email apologizing after I complained and still waiting to leave DFW, it's now over an hour and a half delayed. AA is trash, customer service is even worse, and they have staff answering the phones who have no clue how to resolve any problems whatsoever.

My recent experience with American Airlines was extremely disappointing and far below the standard I expect as a paying customer. My checked baggage was damaged, and items were missing, yet the claims process has been frustrating, slow, and unhelpful. Despite multiple attempts to follow up, communication has been delayed or ignored, and the replacement bag offered was unacceptable in terms of quality and value. I was even referred to a third-party (Rynn Bag Service) without any meaningful support or flexibility in resolving the issue. Customer service was poor throughout this process, with long response times and little to no empathy or urgency. I've been trying to resolve this since August 5, and I’m still waiting for a fair resolution. I urge American Airlines to take better accountability and improve the way it handles baggage claims and customer concerns. If this experience is any indication of how issues are treated, it raises serious concerns about the company’s commitment to its customers

My flight number 2517 on AA, on Friday, August 21, 2025, departing from Cleveland to Punta Cana with a connection in Charlotte, was delayed multiple times. I urgently needed to get to my destination because it was my mother-in-law’s funeral. I am grateful to an AA agent who changed my flight so I could make it, but my original flight was still delayed despite the fact that the ticket cost over $3,500. On the return trip, the flight from Punta Cana was on time and fine . However, flight 3146 from Charlotte to Cleveland was also delayed multiple times. How pathetic is an airline that tries to charge absurd prices and still delays its flights? If I were flying on a low-cost airline, I could understand.

When I booked tickets I chose seats that weren't premium. On both legs, I was moved to a middle seat even though I had reserved an isle. I was told that since I didn't pay for the seat I could be assigned any seat if someone purchased my seat at a later date. Why not notify me? Poor customer service.

This was an excellent flight highlighted by a friendly and efficient cabin crew. The flight left and arrived on time, the pods were comfy and allowed for a few hours of sleep. The main meal was very good, unfortunately the pre landing meal was a literally tasteless Asian Noodle Salad. The entertainment was great as well. Except for the pre landing meal, everything was near perfect.

We are very disappointed with American Airlines. We purchased round-trip tickets to Canada six months in advance, but one month before our trip, American Airlines canceled our return flight and rescheduled our tickets with extra stops. This completely changed our travel plans. Because of the late notice, we had no choice but to cancel everything — by then, it was too late to find affordable alternatives since ticket prices had increased significantly. Booking so far in advance should give passengers peace of mind, not stress and uncertainty. Unfortunately, this experience was very frustrating and left us with a negative impression of American Airlines.

I am deeply disappointed and disturbed by the treatment my granddaughter received while traveling alone as a minor on a ticket booked through British Airways and operated by American Airlines. What was supposed to be a carefully planned and supervised journey turned into a chaotic, stressful ordeal marked by negligence and poor communication.  Her initial return flight was delayed without clear explanation, causing her to miss her connection. The rebooking process was disorganized and left her stranded for over 24 hours with minimal support and inability to stay at a hotel as she is a minor. To make matters worse, her final leg had to be routed through another city 4 hours away from her home, and no proactive assistance was offered to ensure her safety or re-accommodation. - Lost Luggage: Her checked bag—containing essential items—was lost in transit. Despite multiple attempts to locate it, we received vague updates and conflicting information. It took 7 days to even confirm its whereabouts and get it back, and the lack of urgency was appalling given she was a minor traveling alone. In the meantime, we had to get her essential supplies, but she had to go without her prescribed medication for those 7-days.  The most egregious failure was the lack of accountability and care for an unaccompanied minor. Staff were either uninformed or indifferent, and she was left to navigate, confusing airport logistics without proper guidance. This is not just a customer service issue, it’s a serious breach of duty and trust. - Customer Service Breakdown: Reaching a competent representative was nearly impossible. Calls were rerouted, dropped, or met with scripted responses that failed to address the gravity of the situation. British Airways deflected responsibility to American Airlines, and vice versa, leaving us in a bureaucratic limbo. This experience was not only unacceptable, it was traumatic.  Neither American Airlines nor British Airways met even the minimum threshold. She was given a food voucher and shoed away.

I’ve flown first class many times, but this flight was something truly special—especially for my wife, who was experiencing first class for the very first time. What made it unforgettable was the incredible service provided by our flight attendant, Jade. She was absolutely phenomenal. From the moment we boarded, she radiated warmth and professionalism, treating every passenger with the kind of care and attention you’d expect from a loving grandmother doting on her grandchildren. Her energy was infectious, her attention to detail impeccable, and her ability to make each person feel valued was extraordinary. She didn’t just meet expectations—she soared far above them. I’ve never experienced service like this before, and thanks to her, the bar for our future travels has been set sky-high. She turned a routine flight into a cherished memory, and we’re deeply grateful.

Extremely disappointed with American Airlines. In one week, I experienced four delays, a canceled flight, and a flight that took off and returned to the airport mid-air. On AA2244, the delay was due to a mechanical issue - confirmed by staff and supported by evidence. I was being rebooked and issued a hotel because I was about to miss my connection. Within seconds, the flight was canceled and reclassified as weather-related, and suddenly they refused to provide a hotel. I was stranded overnight and told to cover costs myself. Despite clear documentation, American has denied reimbursement. This is a clear failure to take responsibility. I’ve filed a formal complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation.

I usually fly United, and in the future they'll be getting all my business. American cancelled our flight two and half hours before it was supposed to take off. Our flight from MSP was delayed a little due to weather, and we were told our connections would probably be delayed as well, but we had a 3 hour layover in Dallas-Fort Worth so we figured we'd be fine even if it wasn't delayed. But while we were en route to Texas they completely cancelled it. By the time we landed there was only one flight available and that one was in the middle of their final boarding. We ran as fast as we could but it was in another terminal so we were very screwed. The gate agents we talked to were rude, ignoring people and offering a bare minimum of help. The first desk never asked what we needed, just walked the people before us off to the helpdesk to rebook and left us standing there. We ran around to a lot of different desks and I asked one of them if there were any later flights to Knoxville. She said "oh yes there's one at this gate over here" and when I told her no, that one was cancelled, she looked surprised, said they were always the last to know, and started packing up to leave! No offer of help until I asked again if there were any later flights and she pointed me to yet another desk. The gate agent there was one we'd already seen and she was annoyed when I asked her where to go to rebook because she'd already pointed those other customers there earlier in my hearing. I'm sorry I couldn't remember! You didn't care enough to ask what I needed the first time I was at your desk! We finally found the helpdesk and those agents were the only helpful American employees we met the entire time. They actually cared. The airline kept telling them to clock out while they still had a line of 40 people waiting, and the helpdesk agents were not happy about it. One of them (I wish I remembered her name, she deserves a raise) came back to keep working and I think she might have done it without any overtime pay. From what I heard, there were people having to rebook cancelled flights going all over the country. There could not possibly have been weather everywhere in the U.S. I think American just felt like cancelling. What sense did it make to cancel the later flight to Knoxville two and a half hours early and leave the earlier one alone if there was bad weather? Why didn't they wait to see if it cleared up?  I also mention, the gate agent we asked directions of at Spirit Airlines was so much friendlier. The budget airlines are beating you. Get it together or go broke, American. You will not be getting any more of my business.

Was flying with my 87 wheelchair bound mother to attend her last siblings funeral. We landed in Dallas and told our connecting flight was delayed due to mechanical issues. After additional delays we were told NO FLIGHTS out until the next day. We stood in line for over an hour to obtain a voucher for a hotel and the extremely rude agent told us they were ALL OUT? My mother slept on a bench in the airport. The next morning we were one of the first people in line to get re-booked to get to El Paso. They didn't have any seats available so they re-routed us to Albuquerque, New Mexico where we had to rent a car and drive several hours to get to El Paso (car rental was over $300 PLUS we had to pay for the night we missed at our hotel in El Paso) When we arrived in New Mexico our luggage was no where to be found! After our return home I reached out to American Airlines and was offered miles on both our accounts - seriously where do they think my mother is traveling to again any time soon?! It wasn't until I posted a review on Trip Advisor that I received a phone call from the airlines customer relations department saying "this is their final offer and it's the best they could do" The airlines "graciously" offered us $75 airline credit to be used in one years time - AGAIN, where is my elderly mother going to be flying to?!!! While ALL this has occurred my sister has a flight in DECEMBER that DELTA airlines had to change her time and without asking they offered her miles on her account for any inconvenience that this may cause. NOW THAT'S CUSTOMER SERVICE AT IT'S BEST! American Airlines should be ashamed the way they treated all of us on that scheduled flight but the fact that they had my 87 year old wheelchair bound mother sleep on a bench and then offer us $75 compensation is beyond insulting. This airlines has been in the news for negative customer service for quite a while - My advice is avoid American Airlines at all costs if it is possible. The lack of friendly, compassionate staff it lacking, Customer service is not part of their mission at all!

Our flight was canceled 4 hours before our trip. We were re-charged for seats on our next flight. We petitioned for a refund and were denied. This is the message I sent them.  Your representative called me the night they canceled the flight and then gave me only options that charged more for seats. The agent told me I would get a refund for all three charges, then did nothing. I called week later and another representative told me I would get a refund for all three seats. I got one for one vs three. I called back another week later. This time your representative told me they couldnt help me and the request can only be made online. Not your company will not honor the other two refunds.

Horrible experience traveling with my 87 year-old wheelchair bound mother. We had a two legged flight and our second leg got delayed twice and eventually canceled. We were unable to obtain hotel vouchers because the agent said she simply ran out when I explained my mother is in a wheelchair and elderly she said that the couches are very comfortable. The next morning we got rerouted to Albuquerque New Mexico and we were forced to rent a car and drive 4 1/2 hours to get to El Paso so that my mother can attend her last siblings funeral. However, our luggage wasn’t with us. The airline managed to lose it for a couple of days after our flight was completed I contacted the airlines and was offered some extra mileage in our account for future travel. When I explained my mother‘s age and all of her ailments and said chances are she probably won’t ever be on an airplane again the response I got was no worries. The miles don’t expire

My recent experience with American Airlines was nothing short of a complete disaster. What started as a simple flight from Mexico into Dallas turned into a multi-day ordeal of missed connections, unprofessional staff, and zero solutions. After an hour-long TSA line in Dallas, I missed my connecting flight home. American Airlines rebooked me on a 5 AM flight the next day and put me up in a hotel. Arriving at the airport at 3 AM, we boarded the flight only to be told the captain hadn't shown up! After a delay, a new captain was found, but then they discovered "break fuel" on the ground, leading to another cancellation. Rebooked again, the customer service agent suggested a flight to Chicago to catch my original connection – which I missed AGAIN! Stuck in Chicago, I was put on standby, but American Airlines had overbooked every flight, even paying passengers to give up their seats. Now, on my second unexpected hotel stay, all I can say is this entire mess was avoidable and entirely American Airlines' fault. Not once did I receive an apology or a guaranteed solution. The customer service agents in Dallas were incredibly rude, even telling my teen daughter to "fix her face." I will never fly American Airlines again, and neither should you if you value getting home.

Horrible service people, ladies at stands gave attitude and were making fun of me for having headphones on. Tried telling me I’m on the wrong flight even though my flight was correct and proceeded to give more attitude when I tried showing them I’m right. Then the male scanning tickets was rude and gave attitude when I scanned my ticket when I scanned when asked to. Overall horrible experience due to the workers for the airlines I would not recommend flying with them.

I had a horrific experience with United. I will avoid them at all costs in the future. It started with a flight that was slated to leave at 2:45PM. I got a text informing me that it was delayed until 6 hours initially (eventually delayed 10+ hours). Upon discovering that the flight was delayed around 1PM, I tried rebooking my flight to another airline. However, the customer service representative at United told me that there was a "50/50 chance that they will take your checked bag off the plane because it's hot outside." Basically holding my bag hostage so I couldn't change flights to another airline. After a few additional delays, I eventually cancelled my flight 6 hours before the flight time and asked for my checked bag back. They promised I could get my bag back but low and behold, they sent my bag to San Fransisco anyways. This is hot off the tail of me spending 8+ hours at the airport trying to find an alternative flight or get my checked bag back. It then took me multiple calls across 3 days to beg for my checked bag back in which the customer service team basically told me they had "no power to help me" and just to check my bag status on a website (which showed a timeline they missed and couldn't even stick to). It's now 11PM on the third day and my bag "may or may not arrive today". This is far and away the worst airline I have ever dealt with. I have missed work obligations due to this whole situation and I deeply regret choosing United. I would NOT recommend united if you can avoid it.

Since they are trashed so much, I feel motivated to write this. American delivered a solid product on this flight. Extra special kudos go to the flight attendant (sorry lost her name) who, by herself served 20 people drinks dinner and ice cream sundaes graciously and efficiently. Watching her determined and extremely pleasant work was a pleasure.The food, seats, and entertainment were all fine for 5 hours of flying. On time arrival made it a perfect trip except for the frightening ICE agents who “greeted” our arrival.

This airline is incredibly inefficient and unaccommodating. We flew to a destination with a dog in carrier without issue on a different airline. However, when flying home, American highlighted that they have a strict dog carrier size policy. They are an anomaly compared with other airlines. They have different measurements from Delta, Southwest, and other major airlines. After about 20 minutes of negotiating, they finally let us go. We took pictures, and the dog carrier fit under the seat with plenty of room. American Airlines should really revisit this. American also does a lot outdated admin. They use pen and paper quite a bit and are not technologically up to date. They have even bounced passengers off of planes simply for checking to see if there's room on a different flight.

I was not informed that my flight was canceled. I waited at the gate for over four hours before discovering the cancellation. Had I known earlier, I could have made alternate arrangements. When I called customer support, the best they could offer was a flight two days later. They would not disclose the amount of refund I would be entitled to or the reason for the cancellation, and informed me that I needed to contact customer relations for any additional compensation. They explained that I would have to go to the in-person customer line to inquire about the status of my baggage. After waiting in the in-person line for 30 minutes, I was told to go to baggage claim for information about my bag. At baggage claim, I learned that my bag had been expedited to a flight the following day. I spent the next 11 hours making my way home via train at a personal expense of $250. There is no excuse for putting customers through these hardships. Information regarding the reason for the cancellation, entitlement to a refund, and the status of bags is purposefully obscured. This information should be readily available on the app or at least over the phone. Why should I have to call and wait in two lines to gather this information in a piecemeal fashion?

This experience with American Airlines has been an absolute nightmare, and unfortunately, it’s the final straw for us. We were flying from Athens to Philadelphia with a connecting flight to Charlotte. After landing in Philly, we sat on the tarmac for nearly an hour before reaching the gate — which already put us at serious risk of missing our connection. Unsurprisingly, by the time we deplaned and got through the lengthy customs process, we had missed our flight. We were then directed to the American Airlines customer service line, where we waited another full hour. Once we finally reached a representative, we were told we’d been rebooked for a flight the following day. No discussion, no options — just a delayed flight and more stress. Meanwhile, we had to pay additional fees for our dogs to stay another night at the kennel — an unexpected and frustrating cost. We were given a hotel voucher, waited 45 minutes for the shuttle, only to arrive and be told the hotel was fully booked. No rooms available. Overbooked. Exhausted and with nowhere to go, we had to pay out-of-pocket for a different hotel, transportation, and meals — none of which were covered or acknowledged by American. The next morning, we returned to the airport early to try to get reimbursed, only to be told, “We don’t handle that here — you need to call customer service.” So we called, and were told that we have to wait until our entire reservation is complete before we can even request reimbursement. Absolutely unacceptable. Once we land in Myrtle Beach, I will not rest until we are properly refunded. The level of inconvenience, disorganization, and lack of accountability is staggering. Do not fly with American Airlines. They have made it clear that they do not value their customers, and I will absolutely be warning friends, family, and fellow travelers to steer clear. Spend the extra money and fly with an airline that treats passengers with basic respect. We will never fly American again.

I was booked first class. Apparently they changed our airplane to a smaller plane so they had to bump people.  I’m 6’ 6” tall. I don’t fit in regular seats. I pay for extra legroom. I was offered to upgrade to 1st class for only $149. I took it. I was probably bumped because I didn’t pay as much as other first class tickets. It was a 5am flight. I arrived at the airport at 3:45 to check a bag. I wasn’t told I didn’t have a seat when I checked the bag. My boarding pass on my phone didn’t tell me anything or inform me I should check in with the gate agent. I proceeded to the gate and was only informed I didn’t have a seat as I was boarding. How crazy is that? Then they let everyone else board and luckily there were a few extra seats so I was given one. It was a painful flight as I don’t fit in the seats because of my height. I should have been informed earlier. They should have moved me to an extra leg room seat instead of totally bumping me. I have Platinum status with this airline. I fly nearly every week. There were so many better ways to handle this situation but they chose the worst. I wonder how they will compensate me. I don’t think it will be satisfactory.

In future I will do my best to choose any other airline but American Airlines. The flight from SLO to PHX was delayed for more than one hour so I lost the connection in PHX, they booked me on the next flight that was supposed to be to leave at 4:45p It is now 6:15p and we are still sitting on a very warm plane waiting for a scratch on the door to be repaired No snacks or any kind of drinks are being offered to us. I asked the flight attendants to explain to me how a scratch on the door makes the plane unsafe to fly and they don’t have a clue.

I'm a American Airlines credit card holder and a loyal customer. I had the worse airport experience in 30 years of flying. Flying from Heathrow on American Airlines, we were checked in to a smaller waiting area before the crew even arrived. The space was extremely hot, people were crammed in and having trouble breathing and there were no bathrooms available. When some of us tried to go to the restrooms after we were told that the flight crew was delayed, we had to try to squeeze past a bunch of people waiting in line in front of the only entrance. When we continued on, we ran into a locked door that would not open. We were trapped in a space that was too hot with no access to any bathrooms. Once we got on the flight, the crew was extremely rude to the customers.

Worst flying experience ever! Going from Houston to Las Vegas supposed to be flying out of Houston Bush and a few hours before we get a message saying that flight has been cancelled. I called customer service they told me the only option is to go and be put on standby but only 1 spot was available. I told them I paid for 2. I was then told they can put us on a flight out of Houston hobby, I said okay we don't have much options than she tells me there may be an additional charge, I was like how we already paid and this is not our fault they finally said they got the fee waived. Plus I lost the money for our parking that we prepaid for since we are now having to go out of a different airport. We get there and then they delayed the flight to Dallas, by the time we got to Dallas they delayed the connecting flight to Vegas. We were originally supposed to be in Vegas by 430pm we arrived at 1am the next day. Nothing was offered as compensation or anything more like we should be appreciative we didn't get charged more. On our flight back I get a message around 3am saying our flight out has been delayed in which would make us to late to make our connecting flight back to Houston. I again called customer service and we had to get up immediately and get to the airport to catch a flight almost 4 hours than the original one. We were told they would give us priority boarding and 1 free drink for the situation. However when we got to the airport and checked in there was no priority boarding or free drink and did not even have us sitting together, but honestly we just wanted to get back to Houston. I fly somewhat often and by far this has been my worst experience ever. Spirit and Frontier are always the butt of all jokes but besides having to pay for everything on those airlines I have never had these issues with either one of those airlines. I took pictures of the terminal in Dallas on our way to Vegas and the pictures were taken within 2 minutes of each other. Look how empty the airport is and how busy the customer service line is for American airlines.

I used the app to update my seat, and was charged $45, the app informed me that the transaction was not completed but I was charged $45 without getting an update on my seat. I used the chat for 10 minutes a lot of back and forth with questions, btw I answered all of them, and with the same, the person ended the conversation without leading me another way to fix my issue.  I will never use an airline that charges me $$ without providing service.

We had been flying for several years with American Airlines to El Salvador Always had problems with the carry-on, but never wrote a review. This June 2025 was a lot of things: first, at the airport in Raleigh, NC, this lady was taking her time when we were checking in, and we were 3 adults. We got on the airplane at the last minute. At Miami, when we were about to take the second plane, they asked early if anyone wanted to bring their carry-on to be put with the other luggage for free, and this is always. I think you have the option to decide what to do, especially if you have fragile stuff in your carry-on. A staff who thought we didn't speak English, just made signs with his hands to move us to the side of the line. He printed the stickers for our carry-on and put them in. I told him we are not leaving our luggage because of the fragile stuff we had, and he disappeared and let us on the side by the counter. He came back eventually and took off the stickers from the carry-on and let us go, but with an attitude. We got on the plane, and there was plenty of room for carry-on. I don't like this attitude every time we fly. I am tired and sick of this.

I highly do not recommend this airline. My family of 6 and I flew one way and waited 5 hours for a delayed flight ... the return flight was delayed again and in the end we didn't have time to transfer to another one and again had to wait the whole night (7 hours). In the end we spent the night at the airport with small children, we were given a hotel to which it was unclear how to get there in 20 minutes. They allocated $12 for food for each person .

Last Saturday this flight was delayed by 5 and a half hours, but, not to be outdone, the same flight today from SFO to DFW is delayed by at least 7 hours. The airline cannot seem to keep anything close to a schedule to save its life. I've probably been on 20 American flights at least this year and maybe 3 have actually been somewhat close to arriving on time. It doesn't help that all they offer is cheap pretzels and no wifi. Imo this must be about the worst airline in the US. Strongly reconsider if you want to ever get anywhere on time.
